subject: 947 X12 mapping — IDoc to 947 path drops IT1*ADJ for negative quantities
body: Customer rolled their 947 X12 mapping live last week. Customer support flagged that downstream WMS is rejecting messages with reason code BD (damaged) because our IDoc-to-947 path is dropping the IT1*ADJ segment when source IDoc has MENGE < 0. Reproduces every time. Affects 3-5 messages/day for this customer. Need a fix that preserves IT1*ADJ for negative qty without breaking the positive path. Customer wants ETA by Thursday.

subject: Add new regional carrier to existing customized xCarrier instance
body: Customer needs to add a regional EU parcel carrier to their existing customized xCarrier instance. REST API with OAuth 2.0; docs attached. Required functions: rate quote, manifest, label generation (4x6 PDF), tracking webhook, cancellation. Volume: 2,000 shipments/month at go-live, growing to ~8,000/month within 6 months. Mid-May UAT target.

subject: P1 — SAP S/4HANA → carrier integration intermittent failure during peak hours
body: Customer reports SAP S/4HANA-driven shipment creations failing intermittently 14:00-18:00 CET. ~8% of shipments fail with timeout errors at the SAP Cloud Connector layer. Customer at 92.4% during peak vs contractual 99.5% SLA. Need root-cause statement and fix plan by EOD April 27. Cloud Connector v2.16.4. Carriers affected: FedEx, UPS, DHL EU.

subject: Logistics microservice — RabbitMQ consumer memory leak
body: Parcel-execution microservice (Java 1.8 + WebLogic) shows steady RSS growth in production over 72-hour windows. Suspect leak in the RabbitMQ consumer thread pool. Restart resolves; leak recurs. Want a heap dump analysis + a proper fix without a full WebLogic-to-Tomcat migration in scope.

subject: Intelligent Document Extraction — accuracy drop on multi-page PO scans
body: Internal QA flagged that extraction accuracy on multi-page purchase orders dropped from 94% to 81% in the last release window. Single-page accuracy unaffected. Suspect a regression in the page-stitching step. Need a reproducer + fix; have a customer demo on May 5.

subject: Supplier Portal Oracle → PostgreSQL — migration analysis and planning
body: Need an analysis and planning pass for migrating the Supplier Portal database from Oracle to PostgreSQL. Inventory schema objects (tables, sequences, indexes, views, PL/SQL packages, procedures, triggers) and call out Oracle-specific features that won't port cleanly — PL/SQL, hierarchical queries (CONNECT BY), NUMBER/VARCHAR2 type mapping, DUAL, partitioning, sequence vs IDENTITY behaviour. Recommend tooling (ora2pg vs pgloader vs AWS DMS), produce a phased migration plan with effort estimate and a risk register. Output should be enough to scope a follow-on POC and the broader migration plan.

subject: Cloud SRE — release report card automation
body: Want to automate the end-of-release report card. Pull JIRA ticket counts by team, deploy success rate from CI, on-call paging counts, and SLO burn rate, into a single weekly report. Currently this is 4 hours of manual stitching every Friday. Same shape as the existing Jira→GitHub repo migration skill.

subject: BRD + test cases for FedEx REST API integration in SAP
body: Customer change request: integrate the new FedEx REST API into their SAP shipping module. Need a BRD covering scope, dependencies, error handling, and a test case matrix. Estimate target: bottom-up by phase (discovery / build / test / deploy / support). PR-to-SOW workflow as usual.

subject: Customized xCarrier deployment — SSO token mismatch after upgrade
body: Customer's customized xCarrier instance fails SSO after the latest engine upgrade. Token issuer changed in the upstream config, but the customer-side IdP wasn't updated in sync. Need a verified rollback OR a fix to the IdP mapping. Production access required to verify.

subject: X12 850 — vendor sending non-standard ST segment, fails parser
body: Vendor's X12 850 messages include an ST segment with a non-standard transaction set ID (0850 instead of 850). Our parser strict-mode rejects them. Vendor refuses to change. Need either a tolerant parsing mode OR a pre-processor that normalizes incoming envelopes before mapping.
